title: iconHi-C Protocol (ver. 1.0)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"aoir3","text":"Hi-C, a derivative of chromosome conformation capture (3C) targeting the whole genome, was originally developed as a means for characterizing chromatin conformation and is recently employed widely in scaffolding nucleotide sequences obtained by de novo genome sequencing. To gain insights into the best practice of Hi-C scaffolding, we performed a multifaceted methodological comparison and optimized various factors during sample preparation, sequencing, and computation. As a result, we have identified some key factors for efficient Hi-C data acquisition, including preparation of tissues, library preparation conditions, and quality controls of prepared samples. By incorporating those optimal conditions revealed by our experience, we release this customized protocol designated the ‘inexpensive and controllable Hi-C (iconHi-C) protocol’.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":245,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Dissociate cells, count cell number, and collect 1 ×10 7 cells in a microtube (1.5 or 2.0 ml).
Centrifuge the cells. Centrifugation speed: Centrifugation time: 00:05:00 Centifugation temperature: 4 °C

Follow sample preparation procedure that matches the sample type; dissociated cells (e.g., cultured cells and nucleated red blood cells), fibrous tissue (e.g. muscle, kidney, intestine, heart), or soft tissue (e.g., liver, brain, testis, and embryonic tissues).
Take the sample tube out from the ice bucket (from Step 5) and add 1 mL - 1.5 mL, ice-cold fixing solution .
Prepare the fixing solution and put on ice. %s %s Fixing solution (10 ml) (final) 16% formaldehyde solution 1 ml (1%) PBS (-) 15 ml total 16 ml Make fresh every time.
Incubate the cell-suspension for 00:10:00 in a heat block or a water bath set at 25 °C .
Quick spin the sample tube.
Centrifuge the sample tube. Centrifugation speed: Centrifugation time: 00:05:00 Centrifugation temperature: 4 °C
Resuspend the pellet in 1 mL - 1.5 mL, ice-cold PBS and aliquot the cell-suspension into microtubes (1.5 ml) at 1-2 ×10 6 cells/tube.
Centrifuge the sample tubes. Centrifugation speed: Centrifugation time: 00:05:00 Centrifugation temperature: 4 °C
Dissect and collect tissue in a microtube (1.5 or 2.0 ml) and freeze immediately in liquid nitrogen. Store the frozen tissue in an ultra-low temperature freezer (e.g., -80 °C ).
Pre-cool a mortar and a pestle in an ultra-low temperature freezer (e.g., -80 °C ) for at least 01:00:00 .
Pour liquid nitrogen into the pre-cooled mortar with a pestle.

Transfer tissue (up to 1 cm 3 in size) into the mortar and grind the tissue until it becomes a fine powder. Pour liquid nitrogen into the mortar occasionally, to keep the motar and the pestle cold during the grinding process.
Resuspend the powderized tissue in liquid nitrogen and transfer the suspension into a 50 ml tube pre-cooled with liquid nitrogen.
Close the screw cap loosely.
Proceed immediately to cell fixation (Step 11) or store the powderized tissue in an ultra-low temperature freezer (e.g., -80 °C ) until use.
Prepare fixing solution and put on ice. %s %s (final) 16% formaldehyde solution 1 ml (1%) PBS (-) 15 ml total 16 ml Make fresh every time.
Take the sample tube out from the ultra-low temperature freezer (from Step 10) and immediately add 10 mL - 15 mL, ice-cold fixing solution .
Incubate the tissue suspension for 00:15:00 - 00:20:00 in a heat block or a water bath set at 25 °C .
Quick spin the sample tube.
Centrifuge the sample tube. Centrifugation speed: Centrifugation time: 00:05:00 Centrifugation temperature: 4 °C

Resuspend the pellet in 1 mL - 5 mL, ice-cold PBS and aliquot the tissue-suspension evenly into five to ten microtubes (1.5 ml). Aliquot a 4 mm 3 -size tissue into five to ten microtubes. Adjust the number of microtubes proportionally for a tissue at different size.
Centrifuge the sample tubes. Centrifugation speed: Centrifugation time: 00:05:00 Centrifugation temperature: 4 °C
Pre-cool the Tokken stainless-steel tubes and bullets in liquid nitrogen.
Transfer tissue (up to 4 mm 3 in size) into each stainless-steel tube. Break a large tissue into small pieces if necessary. Do not let the tissue thaw during the powderization process.
Put a bullet in the stainless-steel tube and close the screw cap.
Assemble the Tokken tube holder with three stainless-steel tubes. The tube holder should always be assembled with three stainless-steel tubes to balance the weight.
Submerge the tube holder (with tubes) in liquid nitrogen and wait ~ 00:01:00 .
Take out the tube holder (with tubes) from liquid nitrogen and immediately place inside the Tokken acryl-grinder, then vigorously shake the grinder for about 100 strokes to powderize the tissue.

Remove the bullet and collect the powderized tissue in a microtube (1.5 or 2.0 ml) pre-cooled in liquid nitrogen. Hand warm the lid of the deep-frozen microtube before closing its lid; otherwise the tube may break.
Proceed immediately to cell fixation (Step 11) or store the powderized tissue in an ultra-low temperature freezer (e.g., -80 °C ) until use.
Prepare the fixing solution and put on ice. %s %s (final) 16% formaldehyde solution 1 ml (1%) PBS (-) 15 ml total 16 ml Make fresh every time.
Take a sample tube (microtube) out from the ultra-low temperature freezer, immediately add 1 mL - 1.5 mL, ice-cold fixing solution .
Incubate the tissue-suspension for 00:15:00 - 00:20:00 in a heat block or a water bath set at 25 °C .

The following procedure should be performed as described in the step-case for the dissociated cells. Start from Section 7. (DAY3) Hi-C DNA QC (QC1), Step 109 .

Pre-cool the Tokken stainless-steel tubes and bullets in liquid nitrogen.
